text:
Olympics



The Olympic path for the U.S. women’s national team was officially confirmed on Tuesday with Zambia completing Group B following their win in the CAF Women’s Olympic qualifying tournament. Zambia defeated Morocco 3-2 on aggregate after winning 2-0 in extra time of the second leg.Barbra Banda scored both goalsfor the Copper Queens, including a penalty kick in extra time.

Advertisement

In addition to Zambia, the U.S. will face Germany and Australia in this summer’s group stage, playing matches in Marseille and Nice. The tournament will be the first under incoming head coach Emma Hayes, following her debut on the touchline at a pair of friendlies in June.

Absolutely clutch 🔥@BarbraBanda11scores both goals in Zambia’s 2-0 win over Morocco to send them to the 2024 Olympics 🇿🇲

📸@Copper_Queenspic.twitter.com/KIt3xftohk

— Orlando Pride (@ORLPride)April 10, 2024



U.S. interim head coach confirmed after the draw that she will be heading to England to meet face to face with Hayes, and that the two will discuss the team’s approach for theOlympics.



As for the draw itself, the smaller field of teams participating in the Olympics means none of the three groups drawn last month present an easy path to the knockout stages.

The USWNT will open their tournament against Zambia or Morocco at Stade de Nice on Thursday, July 25 at 9 p.m. local (4 p.m. ET/1 p.m. PT). They’ll then shift to Marseille for the next two group stage games at Stade de Marseille, first against Germany on Sunday, July 28 at 9 p.m. local (4 p.m. ET/1 p.m. PT), then against Australia on Wednesday, July 31 at 7 p.m. local (2 p.m. ET/11 a.m. PT).

If they finish first in the group, they’d head to Paris for the quarterfinals, but if they finish second they’d stay in Marseille for their first knock-out stage match. (The top two third-place teams will also advance to the quarterfinals, with those matches staged in Lyon and Nantes.)

GO DEEPER

Macario's stellar return continues - so when will she start for Chelsea?

The USWNT, as one of the three top-ranked teams in the tournament despite recently falling to fourth on the FIFA World Rankings for the first time ever, was placed in Pot 1 alongside France (hosts, no. 3) and Spain (no. 1). According to the draw procedures, that guaranteed the U.S. would either be drawn into the first position in Group B or C — and that they would have to face Germany, as they could not be drawn into the same group as either Spain or France to avoid the same confederation.

Advertisement

The USWNT could also not be drawn into the same group as fellow CONCACAF nation Canada, who will face France, Colombia and New Zealand in Group A.

Zambia was the final team to qualify for the tournament as both African nations represented were determined after the draw on March 20.

Tournament hosts France headline Group A and open the Games in Lyon against Colombia, the same stadium where the USWNT won the 2019 World Cup. Spain, fresh off their 2023 World Cup win, will make their Olympic debut against Japan in Nantes.

Speaking with media after the game, interim head coach Twila Kilgore said she will head to England on Thursday to meet with Emma Hayes this week.

“Fortunately, because I’m in Europe, this collaboration will be face-to-face which is great, but spending time with Emma and especially now that we know who our opponents are, next steps for the group, next steps for the staff, is really important,” Kilgore said.

Kilgore also said that the United States’ path through Group B was the one they were rooting for.

“In terms of location and the sites of games, it’s a really good fit for us for a lot of different reasons,” she said. “It’s the right type of travel.”

Just like the 2019 World Cup (and the Tokyo Olympics), the summer heat could end up being hard to ignore, but Kilhore pointed to the team’s high performance and medical staffs.

“Everybody’s got to cope with it, our opponents have to cope with it too,” she said. “This is part of who we are, that we take adversity head on, we’ll control the controllables, and move forward.”

Mostly, Kilgore was relieved to have the actual path forward for the USWNT, especially as they had already anticipated a group-stage match against Germany. The group stage being set not only allows the team to begin scouting and other preparations from a technical sense, but from a logistical one.

title: Zambia completes USWNT Olympic group, joining Germany and Australia - The Athletic
author: Meg Linehan and The Athletic Staff
